key_insight: HVAF-sprayed Tribaloy-400 forms a denser, more protective oxide layer than detonation-sprayed T-400, giving better resistance to molten solar-salt corrosion at 500 °C.
engineering_relevance: For concentrated solar power thermal-storage containers, HVAF coating appears more suitable than DS when long-term high-temperature salt exposure and coating durability are critical.
method: Deposited Tribaloy-400 on ASTM A516 steel using HVAF and detonation spraying.
Exposed coated samples to 60% NaNO3–40% KNO3 molten solar salt at 500 °C for up to 1000 h.
Compared phase evolution, elemental mapping, SEM microstructure, mass gain, and spallation behavior.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that HVAF retained a uniform Cr–Mo–Si–O enriched surface with minimal salt penetration after 1000 h, while DS showed deeper ingress, higher mass gain, and more spallation.
watch_item: Performance is demonstrated under one salt chemistry and temperature, so coating selection may change with different CSP operating conditions or thermal cycling.
paper_title: Hot corrosion behavior of thermal sprayed using HVAF and DS techniques with Tribaloy-400 coatings for solar energy storage container
